title: Divider
description: Divider separates content into clearer groups without adding heavy visual chrome. Use it to split sections, inline actions, or alternative paths such as auth options and toolbar groups.
intro: Divider renders a semantic separator with horizontal or vertical orientation, optional text, and configurable text alignment. It should support layout comprehension quietly rather than become a visual focal point.

featureSections.title: Basic separators
featureSections.description: Use a plain divider when the layout already provides enough context and the separator only needs to suggest a new block or pause in reading.

featureSections.title: Horizontal and vertical orientation
featureSections.description: Horizontal dividers split stacked content; vertical dividers separate side-by-side tools, metadata, or compact action groups.

featureSections.title: Text alignment
featureSections.description: Start, center, and end alignment help when the label should visually attach to surrounding content instead of floating neutrally in the middle.

featureSections.title: Alternative path split
featureSections.description: The classic `OR` divider works best when two actions are truly equivalent alternatives, such as email sign-in versus SSO or magic link entry.

featureSections.title: Sectioned panels
featureSections.description: Inside drawers, dialogs, and cards, dividers help break longer panels into digestible groups without introducing separate containers everywhere.

featureSections.title: Toolbar and metadata grouping
featureSections.description: Vertical dividers are useful in dense horizontal layouts where spacing alone is not enough to explain grouping.

accessibility.title: Role and orientation
accessibility.body: `DividerComponent` renders with `role="separator"` and exposes `aria-orientation` so assistive technologies understand whether it separates horizontal or vertical content.

accessibility.title: Accessible label
accessibility.body: If `ariaLabel` is provided, it is used directly. Otherwise the component falls back to divider text when present, or to a generic localized divider label.

accessibility.title: Applicability
accessibility.body: Divider is purely structural and not interactive, so there is no keyboard section. Add visible text only when it adds meaning, not as decoration.

apiSections.title: DividerComponent - ui-divider
apiSections.lead: Standalone structural separator for layouts, action groups, and labeled content boundaries. It keeps the API intentionally small: orientation, optional text, text alignment, and accessible labeling.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Controls whether the divider separates stacked or side-by-side content.
Positions divider text when text is present.
Optional visible label such as `OR`, `Details`, or `Section`. When empty, the divider renders as a plain line.
Explicit accessible label override when the default fallback is not descriptive enough.

paragraphs: When text is empty, the divider renders as a full uninterrupted line.
When ariaLabel is empty, the component falls back to visible text or a localized generic divider label.

blocks.title: Related mechanisms
items: Orientation and Alignment - shared unions used across layout-oriented components.
Shared i18n service - provides the fallback localized aria label when no explicit text or label is supplied.
